zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Carminow是Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc和Jane Hill的女兒，出生在英國可瓦爾波康諾可。她和Sir Hugh Courtenay至少有3個兒子和4個女兒。她去世於大約1500年，埋葬在英國英格蘭戴文托利其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Carminow是Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc和Jane Hill的女儿，出生在英国可瓦爾波康諾可。她和Sir Hugh Courtenay至少有3个儿子和4个女儿。她去世于大约1500年，埋葬在英国英格兰戴文托利其。

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Carminow was born in Boconnoc, Cornwall, England as the daughter of Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc and Jane Hill. She had at least 3 sons and 4 daughters with Sir Hugh Courtenay. She died about 1500, and was buried in Torridge, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Carminow est née à Boconnoc, Cornouailles, Angleterre, fille de Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc et Jane Hill. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles avec Sir Hugh Courtenay. Elle est décédée environ 1500, et a été enterrée à Torridge, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Carminow wurde in Boconnoc, Cornwall, England, als Tochter von Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc und Jane Hill geboren. Sie hatte mit Sir Hugh Courtenay mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb um 1500 und wurde in Torridge, Devon, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Carminow è nata il nel Boconnoc, Cornwall, England come figlia di Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc e Jane Hill. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie con Sir Hugh Courtenay. Morì circa 1500, e fu sepolta a Torridge, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret CarminowはイギリスコルンワルボコノックでThomas Carminow of BoconnocとJane Hillの娘として生まれた。彼女はSir Hugh Courtenayとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼女は約1500年に死去し，イギリスイギリスデボントリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Carminow(은)는 영국 코른월 보코노크에서 Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc(와)과 Jane Hill의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir Hugh Courtenay(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1500년 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 토리지에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Carminow nasceu em Boconnoc, Cornwall, Inglaterra filha de Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc e Jane Hill. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas com Sir Hugh Courtenay. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1500, e foi sepultada em Torridge, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Carminow родилась в местности боконнок, корнвалл, Англия. Её родителей звали Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc и Jane Hill. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери от Sir Hugh Courtenay. Она умерла около 1500 г. и была похоронена в местности торридж, девон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Carminow nació en Boconnoc, Cornwall, Inglaterra como hija de Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc y Jane Hill. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas con Sir Hugh Courtenay. Murió aproximadamente 1500, y fue sepultada en Torridge, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
